sections.paragraph: m. שְׁמִינִית f. ordinal (from שְׁמֹנֶה) eighth, Exo 22:29 Lev 9:1, etc. Fem. שְׁמִינִית octave, in music a word denoting the lowest and gravest note sung by men’s voices (basso), opp. to עֲלָמֹות (which see); see 1Ch 15:21, and Psa 6:1 12:1 (where some incorrectly understand an instrument).
